schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: THE OUTER-ROW PROJECTOR: the single owner-gated door that compiles the `intentions/` MODEL as a deterministic, lossless projection of its two source trees — `CairnCommons/intentions-other/` (the homeless intentions: the roots, concept-pieces, host-seams, spanning ones) PLUS the beside-code charters at `cairn/*/intention+why.json`. It reads both trees, embeds each source's content verbatim (a .json source as its parsed object, prose as text), orders the whole set by each source's BIRTH timestamp, and writes ONE artifact — `intentions/_model.json`. A pure core (`compile_model(sources)`) does the ordering + projection with no disk or git; a thin file layer (`gather_sources`, `_born`, `compile_to_disk`) is the today-shape. The single write-door is `compile_to_disk`; nothing else writes the compiled view, and the store's hand-authored `_charter+why.json` is never touched.
why: The seed claim of the `intentions/` store (its charter): take that ONE folder to a bare machine and regrow the system — which only works if the view compiles over BOTH sources, because the beside-code charters alone regrow a repo that does not RUN (everything that made a machine ready lives in `intentions-other/`). That demands a compiled view (Law 1 — the system-scale question 'what does this system intend' answered from structure, not re-derived by walking every file), written through one door (Law 6), that never drifts from its sources (Law 7 — the model is the view, the sources are the truth; a hand-edit is reverted on the next compile) and drops nothing (Law 8 — a lossless projection a hollow build could not fake). It is the OUTER row of the three-scale projector recursion (ticket: history→state; component: design→charter; SYSTEM: all sources→here) — the same move as `cairn/charter/projector.py`, one corpus out.
invoke: Hand-crank: `python3 -m cairn.intentions_model_compiler.compiler` (compiles the real repo → `intentions/_model.json`, prints the count + the roots that surfaced first). In code: `from cairn.intentions_model_compiler import compiler; compiler.compile_to_disk()` (roots auto-derived from `__file__`; override `commons_root`/`code_root`/`out_path` in proofs). Pure core, no disk: `compiler.compile_model(sources)` where each source is `{id, kind, source, born, content}`. Prove: `python3 cairn/intentions_model_compiler/proofs/test_compiler.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: code-seam — the compile-door child (a) of ticket intentions-model-compiler, now also carrying the WRITE GATE child (c): a code-seam script (recompile_gate.sh, git-tracked, proven) plus a host-seam wiring (a FileChanged hook in .claude/settings.local.json, instance-space, not git). Berths in cairn/ (class-space code); the folder it writes is CairnCommons/intentions/. Kin to cairn/charter (the projector), cross-referenced not merged.

host_seam.what: the WRITE GATE child (c) — the LIVE recompile-on-change trigger: when a source changes on disk, a host FileChanged hook pokes the compile door so intentions/_model.json regenerates with no human running anything (shrinking-footprint: the write IS the event, no poller). The gate COMMAND (recompile_gate.sh) is class-space/git and proven; the HOOK WIRING is host-side, where git cannot see it.
host_seam.apply: add a FileChanged hook to .claude/settings.local.json (or ~/.claude/settings.json) whose matcher catches the source filenames and whose command runs recompile_gate.sh — e.g. matcher `intention.why.json` (regex; `.` matches the `+` and the dots) with command `$HOME/dev/src/cairn/cairn/intentions_model_compiler/recompile_gate.sh`. After writing it, the settings watcher may need a `/hooks` open (or restart) to load the config for a session that started before the file existed.
host_seam.verify: hand-edit a beside-code intention+why.json (or touch one) and confirm CairnCommons/intentions/_model.json regenerates — the hook FIRING on a disk change is host behavior, provable only out of turn. The seal expires on host drift (a changed settings.json, a moved repo, a different machine) with nothing in git changing. The gate COMMAND itself is proven in git (proofs/test_recompile_gate.py); only the wiring's liveness is the host-seam claim.
host_seam.iou: HOST-NOTICING, not owner-EMITTING. A FileChanged hook is the host watching the filesystem, a stopgap standing in for the resting state: the source's OWNER emitting on its own write (the emit-chokepoint the state-machine-physics ticket tracks). This is legitimate host-seam practice (kin to cairn-command's PATH seam — a host dependency named as debt, not pretended into physics), and it retires when the owner-gate emit lands. NAMED so the stopgap is never mistaken for the resting state.
host_seam.edges: the matcher covers the beside-code charters (all share the filename intention+why.json); the intentions-other/ half is NOT yet wired — its source names are heterogeneous (telos.md, core-values.md, I-*.md, *.json) so a single filename matcher cannot catch them, and FileChanged's watch SCOPE across the sibling CairnCommons repo is unconfirmed. Until wired, an intentions-other/ change recompiles via the hand-crank or /sorted (child b), not the live gate. Filed, not faked.
host_seam.state: BUILT + PROVEN (command sealed under the tester, VALIDATION at validations/test_recompile_gate.json) + WIRED (.claude/settings.local.json, gitignored) 2026-07-23. The hook's live firing awaits an out-of-turn verify (and possibly a `/hooks` reload).

entry: FRESH build 2026-07-23, to the shape the `intentions/` store charter had already SPECIFIED as its unbuilt projector ('the projector for this scale does NOT exist yet ... a hand-written file here is caught by a reader, which is exactly the discipline-instead-of-physics that Cairn exists to stop relying on'). What is quarried: the projector's pure-core + thin-file-layer factoring and its atomic write-door. What is NEW: two-tree gathering (the seed claim), born-timestamp ordering so the roots surface first BY PHYSICS (Law 4, not a special case), and content-embedding so the one folder is self-contained.
state: BUILT and PROVEN (green bare; sealed under the tester with VALIDATION persisted beside-code). Compiles the real repo's 17 intentions into `intentions/_model.json`; `telos` + the frame (2026-07-14) surface at the top by born-timestamp; both source trees compile in; a hand-edit to the model is reverted and the store charter spared. A MEASURED FINDING drove the born source (Law 3): plain git first-add dated the roots 2026-07-22 (the day they MOVED into intentions-other/) and sank the frame into the middle; `git log --follow` traces past the rename to the true birth (2026-07-14), which is what makes 'roots surface first' hold by physics rather than needing a hand-placed marker. OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) the model is ONE `_model.json` embedding all content — grows to per-intention files or a richer index only if a real consumer needs it (build-minimal); (b) all three triggers now exist: the hand-crank + /sorted, the `cairn compile` CLI face (child b, via cairn-command), and the WRITE GATE (child c) — a FileChanged host-seam poking this door on a source change (host-NOTICING, an IOU standing in for the owner-gate emit that is the resting state; see host_seam); (c) Law-6 single-writer is an IOU enforced by convention until the tester import-scans for a second writer of `intentions/`; (d) `born` from git is the today-shape — swappable behind the pure core, which takes born as data.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_compiler.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND the VALIDATION is persisted beside-code (cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation → validations/<stem>.json) AND committed (git).
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the model is not a faithful projection — a source is dropped or one is invented (Law 8), or content is mangled rather than carried verbatim; (2) the compile is non-deterministic or lossy (same sources → differing bytes — drift becomes invisible); (3) the roots do NOT surface first (born-ordering broken — the frame is not first by physics); (4) `intentions/_model.json` is written by any door other than `compile_to_disk`, or a hand-edit survives a compile, or the store's `_charter+why.json` is clobbered (Law 6/Law 7); (5) only one source tree compiles in (the seed claim broken — a bare-machine regrow would leave half the intent behind); (6) it grows its OWN authoring (content not traceable to a source — the view became a rival record of truth) or its own nightly scanner (that work is the inspector regime's, not this door's).
how_it_learns: Yes — it learns WHICH intentions are load-bearing by making the whole corpus legible in one ordered artifact, and it learns its own drift by construction: because the model is a pure function of the sources, any divergence between what the system SAYS it intends (the model) and what its sources hold is a compile away from being visible — the recompile IS the measurement. Its born-ordering is itself a learning stance: the frame surfaces first because it is oldest, so as the corpus grows the roots stay at the top without anyone maintaining that. Its horizon: whether one `_model.json` stays the right shape, or a real consumer (the web_server's intentions view, a bare-machine regrow) demands per-intention files or a queryable index — firmed up when that consumer arrives, not guessed now.
traces_to: Law 1 (the system-scale model is compiled once from structure, not re-derived by each reader — the outer row of the projector recursion)
Law 4 (roots surface first BY PHYSICS — born-timestamp ordering, not a hand-placed special case; the store charter's 'the_roots_surface_first')
Law 6 (exactly one owner gates writes to the compiled view — compile_to_disk is the sole write-door; IOU until the tester import-scans)
Law 7 (the compiled view is never a record of truth — a hand-edit is reverted; the sources are the truth)
Law 8 (a lossless projection a hollow build could not fake — nothing dropped, nothing invented)
Law 3 (the born source was chosen by MEASUREMENT — git --follow, after plain first-add was shown to sink the frame)
the `intentions/` store charter (CairnCommons/intentions/_charter+why.json — the seed claim + the assembly-point spec this builds to)
cairn/charter/projector.py (the kin — same pure-core + file-layer pattern, different corpus/owner; cross-referenced not merged)
ticket intentions-model-compiler (this is its child a — the compile door)
owner: CC owns the intentions_model_compiler code + its proof and gates writes to them. compile_to_disk is the sole owner-gated write-door for `intentions/_model.json`; per Law 6, a compiled record's own owner remains the owner of the SOURCE it came from — ownership travels with the source, not the projection.
proof: proofs/test_compiler.py — seven teeth a hollow compiler could not pass: lossless + invents-nothing; roots surface first by born timestamp (Law 4); a changed source changes the model; a removed source leaves no trace; deterministic to the byte; one write-door reverts a hand-edit AND spares the store charter (Law 6/7); gather reads BOTH source trees on the real repo and excludes the _-prefixed store charter (the seed claim). Green bare (python3) and under the tester; VALIDATION persisted beside-code at validations/test_compiler.json. The WRITE GATE child (c) carries its OWN proof — proofs/test_recompile_gate.py, three teeth a hollow gate could not pass: it pokes the door (the model appears from sources), it reverts a stale model, and it is non-blocking-but-loud-in-the-record (a failed compile exits 0 yet is logged, never swallowed — Law 7). Green bare and under the tester; VALIDATION at validations/test_recompile_gate.json.
provenance: Born 2026-07-23, built as child a of the intentions-model-compiler ticket cast the same session (from the /intent that bundled a command-runner, an intentions updater, and a 'repo-scanner' — the scanner resolving into the already-held inspector regime, not a child here). Built to a spec the `intentions/` store charter had written in advance ('the projector for this scale does NOT exist yet'). The real first compile surfaced a measurement finding — git first-add mis-dated the relocated roots — resolved to `git --follow` before the seal. The `cairn compile` CLI face (child b) landed the same day via cairn-command. The WRITE GATE (child c) followed 2026-07-23: cast in the primitives sweep that measured the mid-2026 CC feature landscape against Cairn — a FileChanged host hook (verified to fire on EXTERNAL writes, catching a human hand-editing a charter, not only the agent's tool-writes) built as a host-seam-with-IOU, Akien-ratified as the precedent-setting template for mounting borrowed event-primitives (Routines, hooks) behind Cairn's own doors. Still ahead: the intentions-other/ half of the watch (host_seam edges), and the nightly inspector's janitor poking this door on a staleness finding.
